role_title: Customer Program Manager, Executive Briefing Center

level_signals: Compensation range $255K-$320K base, consistent with senior IC at Anthropic
Owns and scales a strategic program end-to-end
Credibility required with Fortune 500 VP- and C-level customers
Interfaces with GTM leadership, Office of the CCO, and executive bench

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Built or significantly scaled an executive briefing program — you have defined qualification criteria, stood up an intake process, and managed a speaker bench.
Fluency with enterprise technology narratives — you're as comfortable shaping the story for a specific customer as you are orchestrating the program around it.
A track record as a strong operator at high volume: many briefings in flight at once, rigorous tracking, nothing dropped.
Demonstrated credibility with enterprise Sales leadership and account teams as an advisor on briefing strategy and account engagement.
Executive presence with Fortune 500 VP- and C-level customers; comfortable running discovery, hosting the room, and facilitating executive conversations.
Strong cross-functional operating skills — able to orchestrate PMM, Solutions, Events, and a rotating bench of executives who don't report to you.
Comfort owning a number and reporting on program-influenced pipeline or expansion to senior leadership.
Clear written and verbal communication; you can brief an executive sponsor in three bullets and write the discovery doc behind it.
Genuine interest in AI and in helping the world's largest enterprises adopt it responsibly.
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

scope.surface: Executive Briefing Center program for Anthropic's most strategic enterprise customers
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B, strategic customer engagement and pipeline acceleration
core_responsibilities: Define and own the Executive Briefing program: briefing formats, qualification criteria, the request-and-intake process from Sales, capacity planning, calendar, and operating SLAs.
Govern which strategic accounts and executives receive a briefing each quarter in partnership with GTM leadership.
Lead discovery with account teams ahead of each briefing to understand the customer's objectives, business context, and where they are in their evaluation.
Own agenda design and co-own agenda development with Product Marketing for every briefing.
Build and manage the speaker bench: maintain the roster, match speakers to briefings, run prep, and manage capacity.
Own day-of orchestration and the customer experience standard for briefings.
Drive post-briefing follow-through: capture commitments and feedback, route them to owners, and track to close.
Build the playbooks, tooling, and best practices that let the program scale consistently as volume grows.
Own measurement and reporting, including briefing volume, strategic-account coverage, and program-influenced pipeline and expansion.

green_flags: Visa sponsorship explicitly offered (with caveats and immigration lawyer retained)
Role has clear ownership and defined scope (program end-to-end)
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit note on DEI and underrepresented groups
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
No explicit years-of-experience floor stated, making it harder to self-assess fit
High coordination burden: orchestrating executives who don't report to you and a rotating speaker bench at volume is operationally demanding

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and program ownership scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed explicitly as KPIs)
scope.business_model (inferred from enterprise sales and GTM context)
location.cities_listed (inferred from 'headquartered in San Francisco' + hybrid policy; no other cities named for this role)
equity_mentioned (inferred from 'optional equity donation matching' in benefits section, which implies equity is part of comp)
ambiguities: The JD title says 'Customer Program Manager, Executive Briefing Center' but the body refers to the role as 'Executive Briefing Program Lead' — treated as the same role.
Minimum years of experience is stated as 'Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' — no numeric floor given; left as null.

The compensation section header references 'OTE' for sales roles but this role is a program management role, not sales; the $255K-$320K range is most likely base salary, not OTE. Treated as base_range_usd.
